description: Distribution market on the US general tariff rate on Chinese imports as of March 31, 2026. The 5-15% bucket is the dominant outcome at 92%, reflecting the current ~10% rate (reciprocal + fentanyl duties). Resolves in 6 days.
question: Will the US tariff rate on China be between 5% and 15% on March 31, 2026?

resolution_logic.summary: Resolves Yes if the general tariff rate on Chinese imports is between 5% and 15% (inclusive of lower bound, exclusive of upper) at 12:00 PM ET on March 31, 2026.

triggers.description: General tariff rate on Chinese imports falls in the 5-15% range
triggers.metric: General import tariff rate on China

exclusions: Item-specific exceptions or increases — this is not the effective tariff rate
resolution_logic.ambiguity_notes: Critical distinction: 'general' rate vs 'effective' rate. General = base rate on all imports. Effective = actual weighted average including product exemptions. A 10% general tariff with hundreds of product exemptions still counts as 10%. Multiple general rates stack — e.g., 10% reciprocal + 10% fentanyl duty = 20% general rate. Current rate is ~10% (reciprocal + fentanyl duties). Boundary values resolve to the higher bracket.

catalyst_dates.event: Any new executive orders on tariffs could shift rate before March 31 snapshot

cross_platform_link.structural_notes: Kalshi has a China tariff rate distribution market but for July 1, 2026 — not March 31. Different snapshot date means different risk profile (3+ more months of potential policy changes). Kalshi's below-10% bucket at 7% vs Polymarket's 5-15% at 92% — not directly comparable due to different bucket boundaries and dates, but together they show the term structure of tariff expectations.

related_markets.notes: China tariff 15-25% on March 31 at 4% — adjacent bucket. Together these two buckets show 96% confidence rate stays between 5-25%.
China tariff below 5% on March 31 at 0.25% — virtually zero chance of de-escalation to pre-trade-war levels
